description: Movement progression chains leading to OP benchmark movements. Each chain defines a sequence of prerequisite movements (regressions) from foundational patterns through to the benchmark entry point.

progressions.description: Squat pattern progression from assisted sit-to-stand through barbell loading

steps.name: Chair Sit-to-Stand (High Surface)
steps.description: Stand up from a knee-height surface (bed, high chair) without using hands. Establishes basic squat-to-stand pattern.

steps.name: Chair Sit-to-Stand (Standard)
steps.description: Stand up from a standard-height chair (approx. 43cm / 17in seat) without using hands. This is the 30-second chair stand test position used in clinical fitness assessment.

steps.name: Air Squat (Partial Depth)
steps.description: Bodyweight squat to approximately parallel (thighs parallel to floor). No external support.

steps.name: Air Squat (Below Parallel)
steps.description: Bodyweight squat below parallel with good form: heels down, knees tracking toes, neutral spine, hip crease below knee.

steps.name: Goblet Squat
steps.description: Squat below parallel holding a kettlebell or dumbbell at chest height. Introduces external loading while reinforcing upright torso.

steps.name: Barbell Back Squat (Empty Bar)
steps.description: Back squat with empty barbell (20kg). Establishes bar positioning, rack position, and squat pattern under barbell.

progressions.description: Hip hinge progression from floor-based glute activation through barbell deadlift pattern

steps.name: Glute Bridge
steps.description: Lying on back, feet flat on floor, drive hips to full extension. Foundational hip extension pattern used in lower back and post-pregnancy rehab.

steps.name: Hip Hinge (PVC/Broomstick)
steps.description: Standing hip hinge with a PVC pipe or broomstick along the spine (contact at head, upper back, sacrum). Teaches neutral spine and hip-dominant movement.

steps.name: Romanian Deadlift (Bodyweight)
steps.description: Standing hip hinge with arms extended, lowering hands toward shins. No external load. Establishes the RDL pattern and hamstring flexibility.

steps.name: Kettlebell Deadlift
steps.description: Deadlift a kettlebell or dumbbell from the floor using hip hinge pattern. Introduces loading from floor height.

steps.name: Barbell Deadlift (Empty Bar)
steps.description: Conventional deadlift with empty barbell from floor (or elevated if mobility limited). Establishes barbell deadlift positioning.

progressions.description: Vertical pressing progression from push-up variations through barbell overhead press

steps.name: Wall Push-up
steps.description: Push-up performed against a wall at arm's length. Minimal load on upper body. Accessible to nearly everyone including shoulder rehab patients.

steps.name: Incline Push-up (Bench Height)
steps.description: Push-up with hands on a bench, counter, or sturdy elevated surface (approx. 40-60cm). Increases load compared to wall push-up.

steps.name: Knee Push-up
steps.description: Push-up from knees with full range of motion (chest to floor, full lockout). Approximately 50-60% bodyweight load.

steps.name: Full Push-up
steps.description: Standard push-up: chest to floor, full lockout, body in plank position. One complete rep with good form.

steps.name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
steps.description: Seated or standing dumbbell overhead press. Introduces vertical pressing pattern with external load.

steps.name: Barbell Strict Press (Empty Bar)
steps.description: Standing barbell strict press with empty bar (20kg). Establishes barbell overhead pressing pattern.

progressions.description: Push-up progression from wall to floor. Shares early steps with pressing progression.

steps.name: Wall Push-up
steps.description: Push-up performed against a wall at arm's length.

steps.name: Incline Push-up (Bench Height)
steps.description: Push-up with hands on a bench or sturdy elevated surface.

steps.name: Knee Push-up
steps.description: Push-up from knees with full range of motion.

steps.name: Full Push-up (1 Rep)
steps.description: One complete push-up with good form: chest to floor, full lockout, body in plank position.

progressions.description: Pull-up progression from passive hang through eccentric and assisted variations to first strict pull-up

steps.name: Dead Hang (10 Seconds)
steps.description: Passive hang from pull-up bar with straight arms, shoulders engaged. Builds grip strength and shoulder stability. Also used in grip rehab.

steps.name: Dead Hang (30 Seconds)
steps.description: Extended passive hang. Indicates sufficient grip strength and shoulder tolerance for pulling progressions.

steps.name: Scapular Pull-up (Active Hang)
steps.description: From dead hang, depress and retract scapulae to create slight upward movement without bending arms. Activates the lats and establishes mind-muscle connection.

steps.name: Eccentric Pull-up (Slow Negative)
steps.description: Jump or step to chin-over-bar position, then lower slowly (3-5 seconds) to dead hang. Builds pulling strength eccentrically.

steps.name: Band-Assisted Pull-up
steps.description: Strict pull-up with feet or knees in a resistance band looped over the bar. Reduces effective bodyweight.

progressions.description: Core and hanging progression from floor-based holds through hanging leg raises to toes-to-bar

steps.name: Plank Hold (10 Seconds)
steps.description: Forearm plank with neutral spine. Foundation core stability exercise used in rehab and fitness assessment.

steps.name: Plank Hold (30 Seconds)
steps.description: Extended forearm plank. 30 seconds indicates adequate baseline core endurance for healthy adults.

steps.name: Plank Hold (60 Seconds)
steps.description: 60-second forearm plank. Indicates strong core endurance. Sufficient for structured training.

steps.name: Hollow Body Hold (10 Seconds)
steps.description: Lying supine, arms overhead, legs extended, lower back pressed to floor. Gymnastic foundation position for all hanging core work.

steps.name: Hanging Knee Raise
steps.description: From dead hang, raise knees to hip height (90 degrees). Combines grip endurance with hip flexion under load.

steps.name: Hanging Straight Leg Raise
steps.description: From dead hang, raise straight legs to 90 degrees (L-sit position). Requires hamstring flexibility and core strength.

progressions.description: Running progression from walking through walk/run intervals to continuous running

steps.name: Walk 1km Without Stopping
steps.description: Continuous walking for 1 kilometer at any pace. Baseline cardiovascular and musculoskeletal capacity.

steps.name: Walk/Run Intervals
steps.description: Alternating 1 minute running with 2 minutes walking, repeated 8 times (24 minutes total). Standard return-to-running protocol.

criteria.details: 8 rounds of 1 min run / 2 min walk

steps.name: Run 400m Without Stopping
steps.description: Continuous running for 400 meters (one standard track lap) at any pace.

steps.name: Run 800m Without Stopping
steps.description: Continuous running for 800 meters at any pace. A meaningful aerobic milestone for previously sedentary adults.

steps.name: Run 1 Mile (Any Pace)
steps.description: Continuous running for 1 mile (1609m) without walking breaks. At this point the athlete is ready for the timed OP benchmark.

progressions.description: Horizontal pressing progression. Shares push-up chain with strict_press progression, then diverges to barbell bench.

steps.name: Wall Push-up
steps.description: Push-up performed against a wall at arm's length.

steps.name: Incline Push-up (Bench Height)
steps.description: Push-up with hands on a bench or sturdy elevated surface.

steps.name: Knee Push-up
steps.description: Push-up from knees with full range of motion.

steps.name: Full Push-up (1 Rep)
steps.description: One complete push-up with good form.

steps.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
steps.description: Lying on bench, press dumbbells from chest to lockout. Introduces horizontal pressing with external load.

steps.name: Barbell Bench Press (Empty Bar)
steps.description: Flat bench press with empty barbell (20kg). Establishes bar path, grip width, and pressing mechanics.
